title: Type Library
text: Type Library A type library is a binary version of an Interface Definition Language (IDL) file, providing complete information about the interfaces of a COM object. It contains a binary description of the interfaces exposed by a component, defining the methods along with their parameters and return types. To make your Uniface services available to non-Uniface development tools, such as, Visual Basic or Delphi, a type library must be generated that contains the interface and descriptor information of the Uniface service and this type library must be installed on the local system for the non-Uniface development environment. Related Links Importing a Type Library To simplify communication with external objects, you can import type library information using the command line switch /sti with /mwr =com. Enabling COM Call-In For a COM client to use Uniface services it needs to know the components’ interface definitions. These are contained in a standard Microsoft Windows type library, which is contained in a standard Microsoft Windows self-registering DLL. The type library DLL must be installed on the client workstation and the client’s Registry settings must point to the server workstation. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
